title: Pointers
summary: Store addresses and access the values located there.
refresher: A pointer holds an address. `&value` obtains an address, `*pointer` dereferences it, and `nullptr` represents no object. Check for null before dereferencing.

blocks.body: A pointer holds an address. `&value` obtains an address, `*pointer` dereferences it, and `nullptr` represents no object. Check for null before dereferencing. Store addresses and access the values located there.

A pointer is a variable whose value is an address. The address-of operator `&` obtains an object?s location, and dereference `*` follows a stored address to reach that object. The star in a declaration says the variable is a pointer; the star in an expression performs dereference.

blocks.caption: The pointer stores score?s address, so dereference assignment changes the original integer.
Check for a valid target before following an address.

blocks.body: The wrong code has no object to follow, so dereference causes undefined behavior. A pointer can later receive `&value`, after which `*score` refers to that value. When absence is not meaningful, a reference may express the relationship more safely and simply.

blocks.title: Inspect this state
blocks.body: An uninitialized or null pointer does not identify a valid object. Dereferencing it has undefined behavior and can crash the program.

blocks.why: The address-of operator produces x?s memory address, which is stored in the pointer.

blocks.prompt: What does `*p` mean in an expression?
choices: the value at the pointed address
blocks.why: Dereference follows the stored address and accesses the object located there.

blocks.title: Pointers checklist
items: Prefer references when null is not meaningful.
